name: X-Force: Internal Connection to Host Categorized as Malware
notes:
This rule triggers when a local host communicates with an IP that is considered to be hosting Malware. It could be an indicator of a Malware or Botnet infection.

Note: The Confidence Factor ranges between 0 to 100. On assets of lower importance, an administrator might weigh an X-Force rule to trigger on a higher confidence factor of 75. When tuning rules, customers should think of a scale where 50 is the tipping point.

See the blog https://ibm.biz/BdPEXv for details.
conditions_text: when the context is Local to Remote
when Destination IP is categorized by X-Force as Malware with confidence value greater than 75
TD_list: APPLY X-Force: Internal Connection to Host Categorized as Malware on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the context is Local to Remote
AND when Destination IP is categorized by X-Force as Malware with confidence value greater than 75
RED_list: Event name: Internal Connection to Host Categorized as Malware
Event description: This rule will notify when an internal system, communicates with an IP that is considered to be hosting Malware. It could be an indicator of a Malware or Botnet infection.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
